proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary clinical and molecular features in ds001_aml, the IDH1 mutation flag (idh1_mutation) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than absence of IDH1 mutation.
Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps, ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: ct = pd.crosstab(df['idh1_mutation'], df['objective_response']); chi2, p, _, _ = stats.chi2_contingency(ct)
analyses.result_summary: ORR is 22.13% in idh1_mutation==1 (n=3624) vs 16.50% in idh1_mutation==0 (n=46376); chi-square p=3.5e-18. IDH1 is the strongest single binary predictor of objective_response in the dataset.
ORR by ECOG: 21.29% at ecog_ps=0 (n=17592), 15.52% at ecog_ps=1 (n=24971), 11.19% at ecog_ps=2 (n=7437). Pearson r = -0.094, p = 6.4e-99. Strong monotonically decreasing relationship.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response (i.e., responders have less weight loss than non-responders).
Higher marrow blast percentage at baseline (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response.
Higher peripheral white blood cell count at AML diagnosis (wbc_k_per_ul) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher baseline C-reactive protein (crp_mg_l) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean weight_loss_pct_6mo: 3.38% in responders vs 3.95% in non-responders; t-test p=4.6e-35. Less weight loss is associated with higher response.
Mean blast_pct_marrow: 58.23% in responders vs 60.31% in non-responders; t-test p=1.0e-19. Lower marrow blast burden is associated with higher response.
Mean albumin_g_dl: 3.825 in responders vs 3.796 in non-responders; t-test p=1.4e-6. Higher albumin (better nutritional status) associated with higher response.
Mean wbc_k_per_ul: 19.07 in responders vs 20.40 in non-responders; t-test p=1.85e-5. Higher AML-disease WBC count is associated with lower response.
Mean crp_mg_l: 5.65 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders; t-test p=2.9e-6. Higher inflammatory burden (CRP) is associated with lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the four classic biomarker-targeted therapy pairs in AML, midostaurin shows a positive interaction with FLT3-ITD (treatment_midostaurin × flt3_itd) such that ORR among midostaurin-treated FLT3-ITD-positive patients exceeds what main effects predict.
Gilteritinib shows a positive interaction with FLT3-ITD (treatment_gilteritinib × flt3_itd) on objective_response.
Ivosidenib shows a positive interaction with IDH1 mutation (treatment_ivosidenib × idh1_mutation) on objective_response.
Enasidenib shows a positive interaction with IDH2 mutation (treatment_enasidenib × idh2_mutation) on objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + blast_pct_marrow + age_years + flt3_itd + treatment_midostaurin + flt3_itd:treatment_midostaurin)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term flt3_itd × treatment_midostaurin: beta = -0.116, p = 0.17. No evidence of synergy. ORR in FLT3-ITD+ on midostaurin = 16.1% vs 17.4% in FLT3-ITD+ off midostaurin.
Interaction term flt3_itd × treatment_gilteritinib: beta = +0.047, p = 0.58. No evidence of synergy.

Interaction is statistically significant but in the OPPOSITE direction: beta = -0.362, p = 0.029. Ivosidenib appears to attenuate, not enhance, IDH1's intrinsic favorable response signal in this cohort. ORR: IDH1+ on ivosidenib 17.4% (n=298) vs IDH1+ off ivosidenib 22.5% (n=3326).
Interaction term idh2_mutation × treatment_enasidenib: beta = -0.135, p = 0.41. No evidence of synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Venetoclax/azacitidine treatment (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine) shows a strong positive interaction with IDH1 mutation on objective_response — IDH1+ patients benefit much more from ven/aza than IDH1- patients do.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ adjusters + idh1_mutation + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine + idh1_mutation:treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine). Stratified ORR computed directly.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.764, p = 4.6e-19 (very strong). Stratified: among IDH1+ patients, ORR is 29.8% on ven/aza (n=1435) vs 17.1% off ven/aza (n=2189), a +12.6 percentage-point absolute gain. Among IDH1- patients, ORR is 16.4% on ven/aza vs 16.6% off — no benefit. Classic synergy pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Venetoclax/azacitidine shows a positive interaction with NPM1 mutation on objective_response — NPM1+ patients benefit more from ven/aza than NPM1- patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.267, p = 4.4e-7. Stratified: among NPM1+ patients, ORR is 19.3% on ven/aza vs 16.0% off ven/aza (+3.4 pp). Among NPM1- patients, no benefit (16.5% vs 16.9%). Confirms NPM1 as a positive predictive marker for ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among 7+3-treated patients, complex karyotype (complex_karyotype) is associated with a lower objective_response rate than for 7+3-treated patients without complex karyotype, with a significant negative complex_karyotype × treatment_7plus3 interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.214, p = 0.0017. Stratified: with complex karyotype, ORR on 7+3 is 15.5% vs 17.9% off 7+3 (-2.4 pp). Without complex karyotype, 7+3 helps slightly (17.3% vs 16.6%, +0.7 pp). 7+3 is not effective and possibly harmful in complex-karyotype AML.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive who receive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than unfit patients who do not receive ven/aza, with a positive unfit_for_intensive × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction.
TP53 mutation (tp53_mutation) interacts negatively with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response — TP53-mutated AML patients gain less benefit from ven/aza than TP53-wild-type patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.103, p = 0.039. Modestly positive: ven/aza confers somewhat greater additional benefit in unfit patients than in fit patients, consistent with the drug's intended population.

analyses.code: Logit interaction tp53_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (adjusted); also stratified ORR
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.112, p = 0.17. Direction matches hypothesis (negative) but effect is not statistically significant. Stratified ORR within ven/aza-treated: TP53+ 16.4% vs TP53- 17.5% (-1.1 pp).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model that simultaneously includes age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, fitness, key cytogenetics/mutations, marrow blasts, WBC, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and the six treatments, ECOG performance status is the single strongest negative independent predictor of objective_response.
In the same multivariable model, IDH1 mutation remains a strong positive independent predictor of objective_response after adjustment.

In the same multivariable model, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a positive main-effect coefficient on objective_response, while the four targeted-therapy treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and 7+3 do not have significant positive main-effect coefficients.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ 24 baseline predictors). Reading coefficient table.
analyses.result_summary: ECOG coefficient = -0.384, SE = 0.018, p ~ 1e-95 (smallest p in the model). Each 1-point ECOG increment multiplies the response odds by exp(-0.384) ~ 0.68. Largest negative independent effect.
IDH1 coefficient = +0.360, SE = 0.043, p ~ 2.5e-17. Adjusted OR ~ 1.43 per IDH1 mutation. Confirms IDH1 as an independent positive predictor.

treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine coefficient = +0.059 (p = 0.016, 95% CI 0.011-0.107). Other treatments: 7+3 +0.017 (p=0.49), midostaurin -0.005 (p=0.89), gilteritinib -0.052 (p=0.13), ivosidenib +0.018 (p=0.69), enasidenib -0.056 (p=0.22). Only ven/aza has a significant positive main-effect; the others are null on average.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, IDH1, NPM1, albumin, weight loss, and marrow blasts, Black patients have a lower probability of objective_response than non-Hispanic White patients (race_ethnicity == 'black' coefficient < 0).
Insurance type (medicare vs private vs medicaid vs uninsured) is independently associated with objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ adjusters + race dummies); reference = white.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race effects vs white: Black beta = -0.108, p = 0.0055; Other beta = -0.194, p = 0.032; Hispanic beta = -0.037, p = 0.28; Asian beta = -0.079, p = 0.13. Black race is independently associated with lower ORR; the disparity is NOT explained by ECOG, mutations, or treatment receipt (which are similar across races).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ adjusters + insurance dummies); reference = private.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance effects vs private: medicare beta = +0.039 (p=0.15), medicaid beta = +0.017 (p=0.66), uninsured beta = -0.004 (p=0.94). No significant insurance-type association after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic IDH1 × ven/aza effect on objective_response is not modified by ECOG performance status (i.e., IDH1+ patients on ven/aza benefit similarly across ECOG strata), so the three-way IDH1 × ven/aza × ECOG interaction term is not significant.

analyses.code: Logit with idh1, ven_aza, idh1*ven_aza, idh1*ven_aza*ecog terms
analyses.result_summary: Two-way IDH1 × ven/aza term remains beta = +0.699, p = 5.9e-11. Three-way ven/aza × IDH1 × ECOG term beta = +0.066, p = 0.46 (not significant). The synergy is robust across ECOG strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is not modified by patient age (no significant age × ven/aza interaction).
Treatment effect of treatment_7plus3 on objective_response is not modified by patient age.
Treatment effects of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and treatment_7plus3 are not modified by ECOG (no significant ecog × treatment interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta ~ +2e-5 per year, p = 0.99. No age modification of ven/aza benefit.
Interaction beta = +0.00076, p = 0.73. No age modification.

analyses.code: Logit interactions ecog × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and ecog × treatment_7plus3 (adjusted)
analyses.result_summary: ven/aza × ECOG beta = -0.031, p = 0.40; 7+3 × ECOG beta = -0.021, p = 0.57. No ECOG modification of these treatment effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effects of the AML treatments on objective_response are not modified by patient sex (no significant sex_female × treatment interactions for any of the six treatments).

The lower adjusted ORR in Black versus White patients is a main effect, not a treatment-specific effect: there is no significant race_black × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine or race_black × treatment_7plus3 interaction.

analyses.code: Logit interactions sex_female × each of {ven/aza, 7+3, gilteritinib, midostaurin}
analyses.result_summary: All interactions non-significant: ven/aza beta=+0.024 p=0.63; 7+3 beta=-0.007 p=0.88; gilteritinib beta=+0.036 p=0.60; midostaurin beta=+0.007 p=0.92. No sex × treatment effect modification.

analyses.code: Logit interactions race_black × {ven/aza, 7+3} restricted to Black/White subset
analyses.result_summary: race_black × ven/aza beta=+0.114 p=0.15; race_black × 7+3 beta=-0.007 p=0.93. Stratified: ORR gap (white minus black) is similar inside and outside ven/aza or 7+3 receipt. The Black-White disparity is a main effect that does not concentrate within any treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, IDH1, albumin, and marrow blasts, the SNP rs1050828 minor allele indicator (snp_rs1050828) is positively associated with objective_response.
After the same adjustment, none of the other 23 SNPs in the panel reach Bonferroni-corrected significance for objective_response (Bonferroni alpha = 0.05/24 ~ 0.002).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ age + sex + ecog + idh1 + albumin + blast + snp_rs1050828)
analyses.result_summary: snp_rs1050828: beta = +0.122, p = 0.0044. Carriers have higher response. Survives unadjusted significance but not strict Bonferroni for 24 SNPs.

analyses.code: Adjusted Logit for each SNP, sorted by p-value
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, no SNP achieves Bonferroni-corrected significance. Smallest p-values: rs1050828 p=0.0044, rs2032582 p=0.046, rs3813867 p=0.053, rs1799853 p=0.064. The remaining 20 SNPs all have p > 0.10. Pharmacogenomic panel is essentially null for objective response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Comorbidity burden, summarized as the count of {diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history}, is not strongly associated with objective_response after adjustment.
Atrial fibrillation (atrial_fibrillation) is associated with a lower objective_response rate, even though aggregate comorbidity burden is null.

analyses.code: Pearson correlation of comorbidity-count with objective_response; ORR by count.
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.0048, p = 0.29. ORR ranges 16.1%-17.4% across counts 0-4 with no monotonic trend. Aggregate comorbidity burden is not predictive of response.
ORR with atrial_fibrillation = 15.64% (n=4143) vs 17.02% without (n=45857); chi-square p = 0.025. Modest negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ordinal symptom-grade variables (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade), none has a strong (p < 0.01) monotonic association with objective_response.

analyses.code: Pearson correlation between each symptom grade and y
analyses.result_summary: fatigue_grade r=+0.006 p=0.21; pain_nrs r=+0.004 p=0.34; dyspnea_grade r=-0.008 p=0.07; cough_grade r=-0.004 p=0.35; appetite_loss_grade r=-0.009 p=0.038. Only appetite_loss reaches nominal p<0.05; none reach p<0.01. Symptom-burden grades are essentially uninformative for ORR in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, IDH1, and albumin, education_years is not significantly associated with objective_response.
After the same adjustment, rural_residence is not significantly associated with objective_response.
Smoking pack-years (smoking_pack_years) is not significantly associated with objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: education_years: beta = +0.0012, p = 0.77. Null.
rural_residence: beta = +0.0014, p = 0.96. Null.
smoking_pack_years: beta = +0.00040, p = 0.43. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable IDH1 effect on objective_response is similar in magnitude in patients aged < 70 and >= 70 (no IDH1 × age dichotomy).

analyses.code: Stratify by age>=70 then compute ORR by idh1 status
analyses.result_summary: age<70: IDH1+ ORR 21.7% vs IDH1- 16.6% (diff +5.1 pp). age>=70: IDH1+ ORR 22.7% vs IDH1- 16.3% (diff +6.4 pp). The IDH1 advantage is comparable in both age strata, slightly larger in older patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous baseline labs do not modify treatment effects: albumin × ven/aza, weight_loss × ven/aza, blast_pct × ven/aza, and crp × ven/aza all have non-significant interaction coefficients.

analyses.code: Logit interactions: albumin × ven/aza, weight_loss × ven/aza, blast × ven/aza, crp × ven/aza (each adjusted)
analyses.result_summary: albumin × ven/aza beta=+0.032 p=0.51; weight_loss × ven/aza beta=+0.0036 p=0.59; blast × ven/aza beta=+0.0020 p=0.13; crp × ven/aza beta=-0.0036 p=0.24. None significant. Continuous labs act as main-effect prognostic markers, not predictors of differential ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The proportion of patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and treatment_7plus3 is similar across racial groups (i.e., differential treatment access does not explain the Black-White ORR gap).

analyses.result_summary: ven/aza receipt: white 40.1%, black 39.5%, hispanic 40.3%, asian 38.9%, other 41.5%. 7+3 receipt: white 45.0%, black 44.9%, hispanic 46.2%, asian 44.1%, other 46.0%. Treatment-receipt rates are essentially identical across racial groups; differential access does not explain ORR disparities.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG performance status is similar across racial groups (mean ECOG by race), so the Black-White ORR gap is not mediated by performance status.

analyses.result_summary: Mean ECOG: white 0.798, black 0.797, hispanic 0.798, asian 0.786, other 0.794. Distribution is essentially flat across races. The Black-White ORR disparity therefore does not reflect ECOG imbalance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ven/aza-treated patients, having both IDH1 mutation and NPM1 mutation produces a higher ORR than having either mutation alone (positive joint effect, possibly additive on the log-odds scale).

analyses.code: Stratified: ORR among ven/aza-treated by IDH1 and NPM1 status
analyses.result_summary: Among ven/aza-treated patients only, the IDH1 × ven/aza synergy (+12.6 pp ORR gain in IDH1+) and NPM1 × ven/aza synergy (+3.4 pp gain in NPM1+) are both observed; jointly the IDH1+/NPM1+ ven/aza-treated subgroup has the highest ORR observed in the cohort. The pattern is consistent with IDH1 and NPM1 being independent positive predictive biomarkers for ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age is not a significant predictor of objective_response in either univariate analysis or multivariable logistic regression.
Female sex (sex_female) is not a significant predictor of objective_response in either univariate analysis or multivariable logistic regression.

analyses.code: Univariate t-test on age_years; multivariable Logit coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate t-test: mean age 68.03 in responders vs 67.98 in non-responders (p=0.72). Multivariable adjusted beta = +0.0003 per year (p = 0.78). Age has no detectable effect on ORR in this cohort.
ORR female 17.09% vs male 16.75% (chi-square p = 0.33). Multivariable beta = +0.025 (p = 0.30). Female sex has no significant effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among AML-related driver mutations (flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh2_mutation, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype), only IDH1 has a meaningful main-effect association with ORR; the others are weak or null on a univariate basis.

analyses.code: Univariate chi-square for each driver alteration vs objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate ORR diffs (mut+ minus mut-): IDH1 +5.6 pp p=3.5e-18; NPM1 +0.6 pp p=0.11; FLT3-ITD +0.4 pp p=0.40; complex_karyotype -0.1 pp p=0.82; FLT3-TKD -0.7 pp p=0.37; IDH2 -0.4 pp p=0.50; TP53 -0.4 pp p=0.52. Only IDH1 shows a strong main effect on a univariate basis. NPM1 and complex karyotype emerge as predictive markers (effect modifiers of treatment response) rather than purely prognostic markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within IDH1-mutated patients only, ivosidenib does not yield higher ORR than ven/aza (reversal of the conventional targeted-therapy expectation in this cohort).

analyses.code: Stratify on idh1_mutation==1 then crosstab treatment_ivosidenib and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs y.
analyses.result_summary: Among IDH1+ patients: ORR 17.4% on ivosidenib (n=298) vs 22.5% off ivosidenib (n=3326). ORR 29.8% on ven/aza (n=1435) vs 17.1% off ven/aza (n=2189). For IDH1+ AML in this dataset, ven/aza dominates ivosidenib for objective response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The most parsimonious description of objective_response in ds001_aml is: a strong negative effect of ECOG, a strong positive effect of IDH1 mutation (especially when combined with venetoclax/azacitidine), positive prognostic effects of higher albumin and lower marrow blasts, weight loss, WBC, and CRP, a small positive ven/aza main effect, and a small negative association with Black race after adjustment — with no meaningful additional benefit from the four targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) at the cohort level.

analyses.code: Final composite Logit with all key main effects and the IDH1 × ven/aza interaction term included; coefficient signs and significance summarized.
analyses.result_summary: Composite model confirms each component: ECOG beta=-0.38 (p<<0.001), IDH1 beta=+0.36 (p<<0.001), albumin beta=+0.12 (p<0.001), blast_pct beta=-0.0057 (p<0.001), weight_loss beta=-0.04 (p<0.001), CRP beta=-0.007 (p<0.001), wbc_k_per_ul beta=-0.002 (p<0.001), ven/aza main effect beta=+0.06 (p=0.02), IDH1×ven/aza interaction beta=+0.76 (p<0.001), midostaurin/gilteritinib/ivosidenib/enasidenib main effects all non-significant, race_black beta=-0.11 (p=0.006). The pattern matches the synthesis hypothesis.
